rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of β-hydroxysulfones from α-methylstyrenes and sodium sulfinates via iodide-mediated anodic generation of sulfonyl iodide, sulfonyl radical addition, and oxidation/hydration of a benzylic cation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to I2 at the glassy carbon electrode; I2 then reacts with sulfinate (TolSO2Na, 5a) to give the sulfonyl iodide mediator (TolSO2I, intermediate I), which homolyzes to a sulfonyl radical that adds to the styrene; the resulting benzylic radical is further oxidized (loss of e-) at the anode to a tertiary benzylic carbocation

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from sulfonyl addition to α-methylstyrene (intermediate II)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I2; I2 reacts with sulfinate to give TolSO2I, which homolyzes into a sulfonyl radical (adding to the alkene) and an iodine radical (regenerating I2 catalytic cycle). The resulting benzylic radical loses a second electron at the anode to form a tertiary carbocation, which is trapped by water to deliver the β-hydroxysulfone.

probe_reagents.name: electron-rich styrene 7d (3,4-dimethoxy substituted)
probe_reagents.observation: Tolerates dehydration; isolated as allyl sulfone 9 in 91% yield, consistent with intermediate carbocation III that can eliminate when stabilized

probe_reagents.name: simple styrene 10
probe_reagents.observation: Gave β-hydroxysulfone 11 in 90% yield, supporting general applicability of the proposed cation/hydration pathway

notes: The mechanism is presented as 'possible' (Scheme 4) and supported indirectly by product distributions: electron-rich substrates give the allyl sulfone via dehydration of the carbocation III, consistent with a cation intermediate. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, CV, or DFT data are reported on these mechanism pages. X-ray crystallography was used to confirm product structure, not mechanism.
